---
title: "between_condition_wilcoxon_aa"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-12
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [26, 20, 17, 17, 20]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot (A-site, P-site, E-site, left-to-right) showing per-amino-acid log2 fold-change of BWM-vs-control median per-replicate frequency. Bars sorted by log2_FC high-to-low within each panel; blue = enriched (log2FC >= 0), red = depleted (log2FC < 0). Y-axis range -0.3 to +0.3.](../../plots/between_condition/composite/APE_aa_BWM_vs_Control_barplot_composite.png)

Each panel ranks the 20 amino acids by signed log2_FC (BWM median / control median) at one ribosome site. A bar above zero means BWM-enriched relative to control; below zero means BWM-depleted. No significance asterisks are drawn — every bar is at best nominally significant and none survive BH-FDR correction.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `between_condition_wilcoxon_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_condition_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):** `between_condition_wilcoxon_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum (two-sided)
- **Design:** n=6 BWM vs n=6 control replicates (control_d0/d5/d10 reps pooled as 6 control replicates; same pooling for BWM); two-sided MW on per-replicate frequencies; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 20 amino-acid hypotheses); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_BWM / median_control).

### Family-level caveats

- **timepoint-pooled-confound** — n=6 per condition is built by treating control_d0/d5/d10 reps as 6 control replicates (and similarly for BWM); if the perturbation's effect varies across day_0/5/10, pooling timepoints as noise within each condition masks signal. Both members share this design.
- **mw-floor-tight** — MW two-sided p-floor for n=6 vs n=6 is ~0.00216; per-site BH-FDR families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) make FDR<0.05 feasible but tight.
- **n=6-modest-power** — Mann-Whitney with n=6 vs n=6 has modest power; null result is weakly informative.

---

## Headline

No statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 across 60 AA-level tests (3 sites x 20 AAs) under Mann-Whitney with BWM (n=6) vs control (n=6) replicates pooled across day_0/5/10. Min `p_adj` = 0.41 at P-site (A and P, both BWM-depleted); the next closest are T@A and R@E at `p_adj` = 0.52. The four nominal raw-p hits (T@A, R@E, A@P, P@P) all sit at the discrete tied raw p = 0.0260 or 0.0411 and are flagged `nominal-only` — none survive BH per-site. Treat as no signal conditional on `mw-floor-tight`, `n=6-modest-power`, and `timepoint-pooled-confound`; the four nominal hits are exploratory leads worth reconciling against the per-timepoint Fisher contrasts.

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` (BWM/control median ratio). `p_value` is the raw Mann-Whitney p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected per A/P/E site (family of 20 amino-acid tests). Each site group is split into one sub-table per sign of effect: positive `log2_FC` means BWM-enriched; negative means BWM-depleted. Within each sub-table, rows are the top 5 per direction ranked by raw `p_value` ascending, with `|log2_FC|` descending as the tiebreaker.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid    | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---           | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| K (lysine)    | +0.157             | 0.0649            | 0.649                |      |
| R (arginine)  | +0.197             | 0.2403            | 0.909                |      |
| G (glycine)   | +0.154             | 0.5887            | 0.909                |      |
| L (leucine)   | +0.104             | 0.5887            | 0.909                |      |
| N (asparagine)| +0.018             | 0.5887            | 0.909                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---          |
| T (threonine)    | -0.122             | 0.0260            | 0.519                | nominal-only |
| P (proline)      | -0.066             | 0.3939            | 0.909                |              |
| C (cysteine)     | -0.019             | 0.4848            | 0.909                |              |
| Y (tyrosine)     | -0.174             | 0.5887            | 0.909                |              |
| S (serine)       | -0.051             | 0.5887            | 0.909                |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| K (lysine)       | +0.151             | 0.0649            | 0.433                |      |
| R (arginine)     | +0.289             | 0.1797            | 0.599                |      |
| Q (glutamine)    | +0.118             | 0.4848            | 0.861                |      |
| I (isoleucine)   | +0.094             | 0.4848            | 0.861                |      |
| V (valine)       | +0.070             | 0.6991            | 0.861                |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---          |
| A (alanine)      | -0.164             | 0.0260            | 0.411                | nominal-only |
| P (proline)      | -0.255             | 0.0411            | 0.411                | nominal-only |
| T (threonine)    | -0.109             | 0.0931            | 0.465                |              |
| S (serine)       | -0.169             | 0.1320            | 0.528                |              |
| C (cysteine)     | -0.126             | 0.3095            | 0.861                |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Enriched (BWM > control)

| amino acid          | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ---                 | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---          |
| R (arginine)        | +0.292             | 0.0260            | 0.519                | nominal-only |
| W (tryptophan)      | +0.161             | 0.4848            | 0.999                |              |
| K (lysine)          | +0.099             | 0.4848            | 0.999                |              |
| F (phenylalanine)   | +0.008             | 0.6991            | 0.999                |              |
| C (cysteine)        | +0.047             | 0.8182            | 1.000                |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site — Depleted (BWM < control)

| amino acid       | effect (`log2_FC`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---              | ---                | ---               | ---                  | ---  |
| D (aspartate)    | -0.114             | 0.4848            | 0.999                |      |
| A (alanine)      | -0.042             | 0.4848            | 0.999                |      |
| N (asparagine)   | -0.040             | 0.4848            | 0.999                |      |
| S (serine)       | -0.246             | 0.5887            | 0.999                |      |
| Q (glutamine)    | -0.165             | 0.5887            | 0.999                |      |

### Flag glossary

- `nominal-only` — raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` >= 0.05. The row would have been flagged significant if tested in isolation, but did not survive BH-FDR correction across its grouping family.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 60
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.4113 (P-site A and P, BWM-depleted)
- `min raw-p`: 0.02597 (4 ties: T@A, R@E, A@P; and 0.04113 at P@P) — the discrete MW tied minimum at this n with ties
- `p_floor`: n/a — user did not flag a floor effect at the CSV level (family-wide `mw-floor-tight` instead)
- Per-site BH families:
  - A site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.519
  - E site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.519
  - P site: 20 tests, 0 hits at p_adj<0.05, min p_adj = 0.411

## Biological interpretation

**The file shows no statistically significant differences at FDR<0.05 across all 60 AA-level BWM-vs-control comparisons (concrete).** No amino acid at any of the three ribosome sites passes BH per-site even with the small family size of 20 hypotheses. Min `p_adj` is 0.41 (P-site A and P), well above any reasonable hit threshold. There is no detectable signal on the BWM-vs-control axis at AA resolution under the pooled-timepoint Wilcoxon design; the four nominal raw-p hits do not survive BH and there is nothing to elevate to a finding.

**Closest-to-significant features cluster at the P site and trace plausible mechanistic anchors, but stay below FDR and are exploratory only (exploratory).** P-site shows the smallest min `p_adj` in the file (0.41 for both A and P amino acids, BWM-depleted at log2_FC = -0.16 and -0.26); E-site R is the strongest BWM-enriched cell (+0.29, `p_adj` = 0.52, `nominal-only`); A-site T is BWM-depleted (-0.12, `p_adj` = 0.52, `nominal-only`). A basic-residue enrichment at the P site (R +0.29, K +0.15) and a paired A-site lysine signal (K +0.16) would normally point at exit-tunnel electrostatic slowing, and a P-site proline depletion in BWM would normally point at modulation of eIF5A/EF-P-dependent stalling — but at these effect sizes (|log2| < 0.3) and `p_adj` values, neither pattern can be asserted from this file. They are directional patterns, not hits.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file shows no significant differences at FDR for BWM-vs-control at AA resolution with timepoints pooled. Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) does the P-site BWM-depletion of P/A/T reappear in `per_timepoint_fisher_aa.csv` at any single timepoint where BWM-vs-control power is not diluted by pooling across day_0/5/10? (2) does the codon-resolution sister `between_condition_wilcoxon_codon.csv` localize any of the same A-site T, E-site R, or P-site P/A cells to specific synonymous codons? (3) does the per-timepoint Fisher show the E-site R enrichment as a real day-by-day cell, or is the MW nominal hit a tie-floor coincidence? Noise to ignore: |log2| < 0.05 cells with `p_adj` >= 0.9 (most amino acids at E-site in particular) — these are within MW's tied-minimum band and carry no direction.

## Methods

Mann-Whitney U / Wilcoxon rank-sum two-sided on per-replicate frequencies, n=6 BWM (control_d0/d5/d10 reps2&3 from each, six total) vs n=6 control, BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site = own family of 20 AA hypotheses). Effect column is `log2_FC` of medians (`median_BWM/median_control`); test statistic is `U_stat`. The test answers "is feature X distributed at different per-rep frequencies between BWM and control replicates?", *not* "is feature X enriched at stall sites vs background" (that is the within-condition binomial) and *not* "is feature X stalling more on day_d than day_e" (that is between-timepoint Wilcoxon).

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **timepoint-pooled-confound.** n=6 per condition is built by pooling control_d0/d5/d10 (and the matching BWM) reps into one bucket; if BWM's effect varies across timepoints, that variation is treated as noise and a real day-localized signal can wash out — a null result here does not imply no effect at every timepoint, so cross-check `per_timepoint_fisher_aa` before concluding biological absence.
- **mw-floor-tight.** MW exact-test p-floor for n=6 vs n=6 is ~0.00216 so per-site BH over 20 tests is feasible only if a feature hits the very smallest p in the family; here the empirical smallest raw p is 0.026 (asymptotic-with-ties territory, an order of magnitude above the exact floor) so there was no realistic path to FDR even before BH.
- **n=6-modest-power.** Modest power at this n means an effect of biologically meaningful size can be missed; a null result here is weakly informative, not strong evidence of equivalence.
- **no-near-nominal-signal.** Only 4 of 60 tests reach raw p<0.05 and all sit at the tied discrete minimum (0.026 or 0.041); relaxing to p<0.10 adds only K@A, K@P, T@P. The amino-acid-wide null is empirical, not just a structural ceiling — there is no near-miss block to argue power was the only problem.
- **weighted_log2_enrichment-absent.** Effect column is the median ratio `log2_FC` with no count-weighted variant, so rare amino acids (W, C with median ~0.5%) get the same column real estate as common ones (K at ~10%); when ranking by |effect| in the sub-tables above, weight common-AA cells more heavily than rare-AA cells.

### Considered and ruled out

- **asymptotic-with-ties.** Concern that scipy may default to the asymptotic Z approximation under tied zero-frequency reps for rare AAs, which would shift p-values away from the exact-floor bound. **Ruled out, empirically verified** (see audit in `scripts/_for_claude_mw_branch_audit.py`): the original "tied zero-frequency reps" premise does not hold for this data — every replicate observes at least 13 stalls of every amino acid at every site (rarest cell: W at P-site in BWM_day0_rep3, k=13/3149), so no per-rep frequency is exactly 0. The audit confirmed 0 of 60 (site, AA) tests have rank ties in the pooled 12-element sample, and `scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu(method='auto')` chose the **exact** branch for all 60 tests; recomputed p-values match the pipeline CSV to ~1e-16. As a counterfactual, forcing `method='asymptotic'` shifts raw p-values by at most 0.015, flips zero raw-p<0.05 decisions, and leaves the per-site BH conclusion unchanged (0 hits at FDR<0.05 either way; the asymptotic branch is slightly *more* conservative, with min p_adj = 0.44/0.61/0.61 vs the exact 0.41/0.52/0.52 at P/A/E). The rule-out stands; the discrete-clustering argument in the prior wording was not the reason.

---

## Plots — individual

![A-site BWM-vs-control amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (R, K, G, L, W, N, E, H, I, F, C, V, Q, M, S, D, P, T, Y, A). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging -0.3 to +0.3. Blue bars positive (BWM-enriched), red bars negative (BWM-depleted). Largest depletion at A (~-0.27); largest enrichment at R (~+0.20).](../../plots/between_condition/individual/site_A_aa_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)

![P-site BWM-vs-control amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (R, K, Q, W, I, G, V, L, N, F, M, H, D, Y, T, E, C, A, S, P). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging -0.3 to +0.3. Largest depletion at P (~-0.26); largest enrichment at R (~+0.29).](../../plots/between_condition/individual/site_P_aa_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)

![E-site BWM-vs-control amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2_FC high-to-low (R, W, K, L, C, G, F, V, I, H, N, Y, A, T, E, P, M, D, Q, S). Y-axis is log2_FC ranging -0.3 to +0.3. Largest depletion at S (~-0.25); largest enrichment at R (~+0.29).](../../plots/between_condition/individual/site_E_aa_BWM_vs_Control_barplot.png)
